title: Designing Sound
creator: Andy Farnell
description: A technical guide to constructing sounds from physical and perceptual models using Pure Data. Examples cover impacts, machines, vehicles, fire, weather, voices, and other sound families.
useful: It teaches how a sound behaves rather than only how to search for or process a prerecorded sample.
audience: Sound designers and audio programmers comfortable with synthesis, patching, and technical detail.
learning: Construct procedural effects in Pure Data
Organize complex effects from reusable components

editorial.note: Included as advanced adjacent craft knowledge for procedural game audio and sound behavior, despite not being exclusively about games.
